content: Walking briskly through the narrow alleys, I suddenly noticed a faint shimmer in the air—a rip, like a tear in the fabric of reality. Curiosity outweighed caution, and I stepped through. Instantly, the world shifted. I found myself lying on a cold metal table, surrounded by gleaming surgical instruments neatly arranged on a chair beside me. The sterile smell was sharp, unfamiliar. Panic rose, but I forced myself to stay calm. Who had brought me here? Why? The tools seemed both precise and ominous, as if waiting for a purpose I couldn’t yet understand. Time felt suspended, urgency pressing down like the cold metal beneath me. This wasn’t just a place—it was a crossroads, a moment demanding clarity. In that silence, I realized the rip wasn’t just in the alley—it was in my life, an unexpected chance to face what I had long avoided.
